EigenInference.claim: EigenInference is a macOS menu bar application that wraps the darkbloom binary with comprehensive scene management
AppDelegate manages activation policy switching between accessory and regular modes based on window visibility
StatusViewModel centralizes all provider state including online status, hardware info, and metrics
Provider status polling checks process running via pgrep and backend health on port 8100
MenuBarView provides the primary UI interface with real-time status and glass effects
ProviderManager handles darkbloom subprocess lifecycle with auto-restart capabilities
Auto-restart mechanism with exponential backoff and crash telemetry reporting
CLIRunner provides centralized CLI command execution with binary resolution
ConfigManager handles shared provider.toml configuration with the Rust CLI
ModelManager discovers MLX models in HuggingFace cache and manages downloads
SecurityManager assesses machine security posture for trust level determination
TelemetryReporter ships error reports to coordinator with structured event payloads
Zero external dependencies, pure Swift application using only Apple frameworks
Wallet and earnings integration through CLI command execution
Coordinator connectivity checks via HTTP health endpoints
HuggingFace integration for model downloads via huggingface-cli

EigenInferenceEnclave.claim: The component is a Swift package with library and executable products targeting macOS 13+
SecureEnclaveIdentity manages hardware-bound P-256 keys with tamper-resistant storage
FFI bridge provides C-callable functions for Rust integration with opaque pointer management
AttestationBlob contains hardware identity and security state fields in alphabetical order
AttestationService creates signed attestation blobs with deterministic JSON encoding
CLI tool provides attest command for generating ephemeral attestations
System information collected via system_profiler for chip identification
Component imports CryptoKit and Foundation for cryptographic and system operations
FFI bridge enables Rust integration through C ABI with documented memory management
Signing operations occur in Secure Enclave hardware with DER-encoded output
Hardware model retrieved via sysctl hw.model parameter
Authenticated Root Volume status checked via diskutil for volume sealing verification
FFI bridge functions tested for identity creation, key export, and memory management
Public keys exported in multiple formats: raw bytes, base64, and hex
CLI provides attest and info commands with optional key binding parameters

EigenInferenceEnclaveCLI.claim: The CLI imports CryptoKit for cryptographic operations, EigenInferenceEnclave for core functionality, and Foundation for runtime support
The CLI provides two main commands: attest for generating signed attestation blobs and info for showing Secure Enclave availability, with emphasis on ephemeral keys
The cmdAttest function validates Secure Enclave availability, creates ephemeral identity, and uses AttestationService to generate signed attestations
Secure Enclave availability is checked before attempting cryptographic operations, with error exit if unavailable
The cmdInfo function reports device capabilities including secure enclave availability and ephemeral public key
Command-line argument parsing handles attest command with optional encryption-key and binary-hash parameters
Usage documentation shows command structure and options for both attest and info commands
JSON encoding uses ISO 8601 dates and sorted keys for deterministic output to STDOUT
Error handling outputs error messages to STDERR and exits with code 1 on failures
WebSocket bridge functionality was removed due to Apple keychain restrictions with ACME managed identities
Package requires macOS 13.0 or later for Secure Enclave functionality
CLI executable target depends directly on EigenInferenceEnclave library
Device info includes secure_enclave_available boolean and key_persistence set to ephemeral

analytics.claim: Entry point orchestrates service initialization with configuration loading
Builds appropriate storage backend based on configuration
Configuration struct supports dual backend modes
Different validation requirements for memory vs postgres backends
Health check endpoint returns service status and backend type
Earnings leaderboard endpoint with query parameter validation
Service layer implements business logic with clean interfaces
Core earnings leaderboard logic with ranking and formatting
MemoryStore contains hardcoded sample data for development
PostgreSQL queries for network overview statistics
Primary external dependency is pgx PostgreSQL driver
Standalone read-only analytics service designed to sit beside coordinator
Reads from providers and provider_earnings database tables

coordinator.claim: The coordinator is the central routing and trust layer in the Darkbloom network that accepts provider WebSocket connections, verifies Secure Enclave attestations, and routes OpenAI-compatible requests
The coordinator runs in a GCP Confidential VM (AMD SEV-SNP) with hardware-encrypted memory and reads requests for routing but never logs prompt content
Server is the main HTTP/WS server that ties together the provider registry, key store, payment ledger, billing service, and HTTP routing with various configuration options
The registry manages the set of connected provider agents, their capabilities, and routes inference requests using round-robin among idle providers with trust level filtering
Store provides storage backends with MemoryStore for development and PostgresStore for production, managing double-entry ledger in micro-USD
Attestation verifier checks signed attestation blobs using P-256 ECDSA keys from Apple Secure Enclave, validating hardware identity and security state
Billing service provides unified payment processing with Stripe Checkout for deposits and Stripe Connect Express for provider payouts
Protocol defines WebSocket message types for coordinator-provider communication including registration, heartbeats, inference requests with optional X25519/NaCl-box encryption
Consumer handlers implement OpenAI-compatible endpoints with sender-sealed requests, trusted routing in Confidential VM, and re-encryption to providers
Payment ledger tracks consumer balances and provider payouts with all amounts in micro-USD, supporting Stripe payments and settlements
PostgreSQL connectivity via jackc/pgx/v5 driver
WebSocket communication via nhooyr.io/websocket library
Datadog integration for monitoring via datadog-go and dd-trace-go libraries, JWT authentication, UUID generation, and crypto libraries
API surface includes OpenAI-compatible consumer endpoints, authentication/billing endpoints, provider WebSocket, admin operations, and public stats
Inference request flow includes balance reservation, model validation, provider routing with retry logic, and streaming response handling
Data persistence supports PostgreSQL for production and in-memory store for development with explicit opt-in via EIGENINFERENCE_ALLOW_MEMORY_STORE
Stripe billing integration with webhook secrets, success/cancel URLs, and Connect Express configuration for provider payouts
Datadog APM tracer integration with service/environment configuration and DogStatsD metrics client
MicroMDM integration for independent provider security verification with callback handling for MDA certificate storage
Trust levels include none (no attestation), self_signed (provider's own key), and hardware (MDM + MDA + SE key bound to Apple-verified hardware)
Inference timeout is 600 seconds (10 minutes) to allow 32k tokens at ~55 tok/s on slower hardware, with retry logic up to 3 attempts

darkbloom.claim: Darkbloom is a provider agent for Apple Silicon Macs that manages local inference requests and handles attestation using Apple Secure Enclave
The component is named 'darkbloom' and is described as an 'EigenInference provider agent for Apple Silicon Macs'
The CLI provides 15+ subcommands including init, serve, install, enroll, models, earnings, doctor, start, stop, logs, update, login, logout, autoupdate, and hash-runtime
WebSocket client manages connection to coordinator with automatic reconnection, registration, heartbeats, and attestation challenges
Backend manager wraps vllm-mlx with health monitoring and automatic restart using exponential backoff
Legacy local-only HTTP server exists as debug escape hatch with OpenAI-compatible endpoints, quarantined behind environment variables
Ephemeral X25519 key pairs generated fresh on each launch for E2E encryption using NaCl Box
launchd user agent management allows controlled start/stop without auto-restart functionality
Configuration system uses TOML format with hardware-adaptive defaults and CLI override capabilities
Telemetry subsystem uses async batching with HTTPS POST to coordinator and disk overflow protection
Uses tokio async runtime with full features for WebSocket handling and concurrent operations
Uses reqwest HTTP client with JSON and streaming support for backend communication and coordinator API calls
Uses crypto_box for NaCl-compatible X25519 + XSalsa20-Poly1305 encryption and base64/sha2 for cryptographic operations
macOS-specific dependencies include security-framework for Secure Enclave operations and core-foundation for system integration
Local HTTP API provides OpenAI-compatible endpoints for health, models, and chat completions
Integrates with multiple external systems including coordinator URLs, R2 CDN, and enrollment profiles
Request proxy handles inference forwarding between coordinator and backend with secure memory wiping
WebSocket protocol includes registration, heartbeats, inference requests/responses, and attestation challenges
Runtime verification system ensures Python binary and package integrity through hash verification
Exponential backoff recovery system with 1s to 5s maximum delay for backend restart reliability

decrypt-test.claim: The component is a minimal NaCl Box decryptor for cross-language compatibility testing with specific CLI usage pattern
The main function handles command-line argument parsing and validates exactly 3 arguments
Base64 decoding layer processes all three input parameters with comprehensive error handling
Key size validation ensures ephemeral public key and provider private key are exactly 32 bytes, ciphertext at least 24 bytes
Cryptographic operations construct SalsaBox and extract nonce from ciphertext
Decryption and output processing converts result to UTF-8 and outputs to stdout
External dependencies are crypto_box 0.9 for cryptographic operations and base64 0.22 for encoding
Dependencies are imported for base64 operations and cryptographic primitives
The component is used for cross-language encryption compatibility testing between Go coordinator and Rust provider crypto implementations
The test builds the Rust decryptor binary and locates it for execution
The binary is executed with base64-encoded arguments and outputs plaintext to stdout

verify-attestation.claim: The tool reads attestation data from a hardcoded file path and handles read errors with exit code 1
Uses attestation.VerifyJSON() as the core verification function and handles parsing errors
Outputs hardware information including chip name and hardware model from the attestation
Provides clear success/failure indication with descriptive messages and appropriate exit codes
Imports the coordinator's internal attestation package for verification functionality
VerifyJSON function parses JSON data into SignedAttestation struct and delegates to Verify function
The Verify function checks P-256 ECDSA signatures and validates security requirements including Secure Enclave, SIP, and Secure Boot
AttestationBlob struct mirrors Swift AttestationBlob with alphabetically ordered fields for JSON compatibility
The package provides cross-language JSON compatibility between Swift's JSONEncoder and Go's encoding/json
Package verifies signed attestation blobs from Darkbloom provider nodes using Apple Secure Enclave P-256 ECDSA keys

web.claim: The component is named 'web' and uses Next.js 16 as its core framework
Uses layered architecture with AppShell, authentication providers, and theme management
Implements Darkbloom AI assistant with hardware attestation and end-to-end encryption
Uses Zustand for state management with persistent chat history and model selection
Supports optional NaCl Box encryption for sender-coordinator privacy
Implements X25519 NaCl Box encryption with ephemeral keypairs for forward secrecy
Auto-provisions API keys via Privy tokens and handles token refresh
Performs in-browser X.509 certificate verification against Apple Enterprise Attestation Root CA
Next.js API routes act as proxies with streaming support and header passthrough
AppShell provides main layout with sidebar and authentication-aware rendering
Runtime dependencies include Next.js, React 19, Privy auth, NaCl crypto, and pkijs
Extracts trust metadata from response headers for hardware attestation display
ChatMessage component renders markdown with thinking blocks and trust indicators
API key provisioning route supports both Authorization header and Privy token cookie
Browser-side telemetry client sends debounced event batches to coordinator
Testing setup uses Vitest with jsdom environment and TypeScript path aliases
Uses EigenCloud brand design system with custom CSS variables for theming
Sidebar provides navigation for chat, stats, provider dashboard, earnings, and API console
Provider dashboard integrates with coordinator API for machine status and earnings
Streaming chat supports encrypted SSE events with response unsealing
